etymology.meaning: to be dimmed, dulled; to be blinded; to

Preterit.turoyo: 060: Hedi gmaḥtiwa aʕlayye ʕafro, ṭino, mgawliwa u ṭino bu ʕafrano. Howewa hawxa falqe, saymiwayle falqe. (2018_07_01_01.2 Arkaḥ, old houses)

translations:
060:Then
			they used to put upon them (qayse) earth, clay, they used to mix
			the clay with this earth. They are resulted in this way lumps,
			they made it (ṭino-the clay) into lumps.

Infectum.turoyo: Kul yawmo u šaġolayḏi kogowǝl ḥamšo kise d xarč w ono gmoḥe<ne>, mǝstaʕmalnalle/nanne (İlyas) bu bnoyo du šuro du bayto.

translations:
‘Our
			worker every day mixes five sacks of cement and I throw <it|>,
			I used them (sacks) in the construction of the wall of the house.’

Infectum.turoyo: İnaqla d kogawlat u xarč, i čǝmǝnto u qum ʕam ḥḏoḏe kxulṭaṭ, xulṭaṭṭe u bǝṯr gfǝtḥatle lu maġǝblo, maǧǝblo u gmaḥtat maye ebe hul gšote am mayayḏe hedi madǝʕraṭle šafiro b ḏukṯe ʕal ḥḏoḏe hul d howe xud u tino towe ṭawwo, xud u ṭino qašyo. (Iṣḥaq d Arkaḥ p.c)

translations:
İnaqla
			d kogawlina u xarč d howe ġalabe zoyuḏo iḏa dlo
			mǝstaʕamlinale bu zabnayḏe xd glozǝm b zabno karyo gnošǝf w
			naqla ḥreto iḏa d maḥtina aʕle maye glayšinale me ḥaṯo
			elo lo kfoyǝš ebe ḥaylo xd inaqqa d bi qamayto mide d gmitaḥat
			aʕle tarte kore maye aw da ṯloṯ bu šǝklano, hawxa. (Iṣḥaq
			d Arkaḥ p.c)
